friction_items.description: The qualitative pack (src/research_os_qualitative/) still registers triggers through router_entries.py (QUALITATIVE_ROUTER_ENTRIES dict, 5 protocols), while core protocols register through src/research_os/protocols/_router_index.yaml. There is no preflight enforcing that triggers do not collide. A live audit found SIX colliding triggers between the pack and core: 'open coding', 'axial coding' (pack:qualitative/coding/coding_scheme_iteration vs core:methodology/qualitative_research), 'member checking' (pack:qualitative/validity/member_checking vs core:methodology/qualitative_quality_audit), 'grounded theory' (pack:qualitative/method/grounded_theory_iteration vs core:methodology/qualitative_research), 'coreq', 'srqr' (pack:qualitative/output/qualitative_report_format vs core:methodology/qualitative_research). Protocol IDs are namespaced (no ID collisions), which is a real improvement vs baseline, but trigger-phrase collisions still leave routing ambiguous and the precedence rule is still undocumented. A maintainer reviewing a PR that adds a new trigger to either source cannot tell from the diff alone whether it shadows an existing one.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a preflight check that fails if any trigger phrase appears in both core _router_index.yaml and any installed pack's router_entries. Document precedence in PROTOCOL_DOCTRINE.md (suggestion: pack triggers win when both match, since packs are opt-in and more specific). Better yet, fold the pack router_entries into _router_index.yaml on install (single registry at runtime) and keep router_entries.py only as a build-time source.

friction_items.description: Tool errors for missing required input arguments leak raw Python KeyError text as the entire error envelope. tool_python_exec called without script_path returns {'status':'error','error':"'script_path'"} — just the bare key name in single quotes. Same for tool_qualitative_codebook_diff called with the wrong kwarg names (codebook_v_prev/codebook_v_curr instead of codebook_v1/codebook_v2): {'status':'error','error':"'codebook_v1'"}. The user-friendly error machinery exists elsewhere (e.g. tool_audit returns 'tool_audit requires scope= and dimension=. Valid scopes: step | project | synthesis. See docs/V2_MIGRATION_TABLE.md...' — perfect maintainer-grade error), but the consolidated tool error envelope is inconsistently applied. The dispatch.py except-Exception handler stringifies the bare exception instead of pretty-printing missing required args. A maintainer responding to a 'qual tool is broken' user report has to read the handler source to know what was actually missing.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Add a small wrapper in research_os.server.dispatch (or a decorator on each handler) that validates arguments against inputSchema['required'] BEFORE invoking the handler, returning {status:'error', code:'missing_required_arg', message:'tool_X requires <args>. You provided <names>.', suggested_actions:['call sys_tool_describe("tool_X") for the schema']}. Refactor handlers to assume validated input. Pilot on tool_python_exec + the 3 tool_qualitative_* tools, then propagate. The pattern that tool_audit already follows is the canonical model — extend it.

friction_items.description: Synthesis surface still has 4 sibling tools (tool_synthesize, tool_synthesize_plan, tool_synthesis_curate_figures, tool_synthesis_preview) and routing surface still has 6 (tool_route, tool_semantic_route, sys_semantic_tool_search, tool_quick_route, tool_research_method, tool_research_tool). These were partially named-out in the Phase 15a 'convoluted_tools' list. The consolidation has happened for audit_*, sys_path_*, sys_config_*, sys_env, sensitivity, preregister, reviewer, data, figure, thought, scratch, task — a huge sweep — but synth_* and route_* still feel like several overlapping verbs. From a maintainer perspective, adding a new synthesis stage now requires choosing between 4 verbs and updating the right one; same for adding a new routing strategy.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Phase 9 follow-up (v2.1.0): consolidate synth_* into tool_synthesize(operation=plan|curate_figures|preview|run) with the same alias-then-removal lifecycle Phase 9 used. Same for tool_route + tool_quick_route + tool_research_method + tool_research_tool — collapse into tool_route(mode=hierarchical|semantic|quick|method_only|tool_only). Keep tool_semantic_route + sys_semantic_tool_search distinct (different return surfaces — protocols vs tools).

friction_items.description: tool_python_exec runs subprocess with cwd=str(p.parent) — i.e. the directory containing the script. This is implicit and undocumented in the tool description, which just says 'Execute a Python script located in the workspace.' Any script that uses relative paths to write outputs (e.g. pathlib.Path('workspace/...').mkdir()) ends up writing under workspace/<step>/code/workspace/<step>/... because the cwd jump silently nests the path. This bit me twice during this validation. A maintainer fielding a 'my outputs disappeared' bug report will spend non-trivial time on this.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Either (a) run subprocess with cwd=str(root) so all relative paths resolve from the workspace root (most users expect this), and update the description; or (b) keep the cwd=script_parent semantics but explicitly document it: 'cwd is set to the script's parent directory; use absolute paths or repository-root-relative paths via the WORKSPACE_ROOT env var (which the runner exports).' Add WORKSPACE_ROOT to the subprocess env so scripts have a stable anchor.

friction_items.description: Baseline named 14 tools that the qualitative scenario never touches. Phase 9/14 removed 11 of them (tool_audit_step_completeness, tool_audit_synthesis, tool_audit_master, tool_audit_step_literature, tool_audit_figure_full, tool_figure_palette, tool_preregister_diff, tool_step_env_lock, tool_env_snapshot, tool_env_docker_generate, tool_paper_figures_autoembed, tool_dashboard_create). THREE are still in the surface: tool_workflow_dag (compute-DAG, no relevance to a transcript-coding workflow), tool_poster_create (qualitative usually ships a manuscript, not a poster), tool_latex_compile (qualitative usually goes through pandoc/typst). Also: 4 adapter tools (sys_adapters_installed, tool_adapter_extract, tool_adapters_list, tool_adapters_run_all) still appear unconditionally in the surface for a workspace with no adapter installed. Minor surface bloat but real.
friction_items.suggested_fix: For tool_poster_create + tool_latex_compile + tool_workflow_dag: move into an opt-in 'publishing' / 'compute' pack (research_os_publishing, research_os_compute) and gate list_tools registration on pack-installed. For adapter_* tools: only register them when at least one adapter is detected in the workspace (sys_adapters_installed != []). Saves 3-7 surface entries for the average qualitative project.

friction_items.description: Baseline (Phase 15a friction #3) asked for protocol_category segmentation in reliability.jsonl + a tool_reliability_summary_by_category CLI flag so a maintainer can run 'research-os doctor --reliability qualitative' and get a 30-day BLOCK / override / abandonment table. Neither docs/MAINTAINER_DASHBOARD.md nor a per-category telemetry dispatcher exist yet. The reliability machinery is in place (testing/reliability.py) but maintainer-facing aggregation isn't shipped. This blocks evidence-based prioritization of which protocol to harden next.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Defer to v2.1.0. Add protocol_category to the reliability log writer (auto-populated from the protocol id, qualitative_* -> qualitative, etc.) and a research-os doctor --by-category flag. Document the workflow in docs/MAINTAINER_DASHBOARD.md. This was an open ask in baseline and remains one.

friction_items.description: Baseline asked for docs/QUALITATIVE_GLOSSARY.md to centralize the ~20 methodology terms (Lincoln & Guba trustworthiness criteria, codebook-TA vs reflexive TA, semantic vs latent coding, etc.) instead of repeating them inline across pack protocols. The glossary file does not exist. Pack protocol descriptions still carry the same density of unexplained jargon. A first-time reviewer of a PR touching thematic_analysis_braun_clarke.yaml or coding_scheme_iteration.yaml has to parallel-read primary literature to sanity-check the change.
friction_items.suggested_fix: Defer to v2.1.0. Write docs/QUALITATIVE_GLOSSARY.md (~1 page, ~20 terms, primary citations once). Add a one-line link reference at the top of each pack protocol's description so the glossary is discoverable. Trims pack protocol description length by ~15-25%.

convoluted_tools: tool_synthesize_plan + tool_synthesize + tool_synthesis_preview + tool_synthesis_curate_figures (4 synth verbs that could collapse to tool_synthesize(operation=plan|run|preview|curate_figures))
tool_route + tool_quick_route + tool_research_method + tool_research_tool + tool_semantic_route + sys_semantic_tool_search (6 routing verbs; the first 4 could fold into tool_route(mode=...))
tool_python_exec (cwd-rebasing to script's parent dir is implicit, undocumented, and trips up callers — easy 30-minute bug to land on)
confusing_protocols: methodology/coding_scheme_development (core) vs qualitative/coding/coding_scheme_iteration (pack) — protocol IDs are now disjoint (good), but the semantic overlap remains and there's no doctrine note saying which to prefer when starting fresh vs iterating

methodology/qualitative_research (core, COREQ/SRQR generalist) vs qualitative/method/thematic_analysis_braun_clarke + qualitative/method/grounded_theory_iteration (pack, method-specific) — the layering is sensible (core = generic, pack = method-specific) but is not stated in PROTOCOL_DOCTRINE.md and the routing precedence between them is data-dependent (which triggers win first)

methodology/qualitative_pii_redaction still references a scripts/qual_pii_redact.py that the researcher has to write themselves — the protocol's quality_bar enforces 18-class HIPAA coverage and NER recall >= 0.95 but ships no first-class MCP wrapper, so the contract is 'do this rigorous thing with your own code'
never_called_tools: tool_workflow_dag (compute-heavy reproducibility tool; no DAG in a transcript-coding workflow)
tool_adapter_extract, tool_adapters_list, tool_adapters_run_all (no slurm/synapse/cytoscape/redcap/nextflow/snakemake in a qualitative project)
sys_adapters_installed (returns empty list for a qualitative-only workspace; could be omitted from the surface in that case)
improvements_vs_baseline: Tool surface count: 211 -> 146 (31% reduction). All 146 carry status='live' and pack=<name> annotations — zero alias/deprecated noise in the surface, zero status-missing entries.

server.py monolith -> modular package: src/research_os/server/{entry,dispatch,registry,aliases,envelopes,rate_limiter,pack_loader,optional_deps,_helpers,_handlers_runtime}.py + tool_definitions/{meta,research,audit,synthesis,methodology,grounding}.py + handlers/{audit_core,audit_gates,grounding,meta_help,meta_routing,meta_sys,meta_workspace,methodology,research_exec,research_search,synthesis_reviewer,synthesis_visual,synthesis_writing}.py. Largest file is now 579 lines (was 7,499); no file exceeds 1,500. Adding a new audit tool now touches one file (handlers/audit_core.py + tool_definitions/audit.py), not a 7.5K-line dispatcher.

MCP instructions field shipped (entry.py line 92-99): on every turn the client sees the canonical 4-step ritual (sys_boot -> tool_route -> sys_protocol_get format=summary -> sys_active_tools). Removes 'how do I bootstrap?' as a discovery question.

sys_protocol_get format default flipped from 'full' to 'summary' (meta.py line 107). Default call now returns ~3K bytes (id + step headings + quality_bar + expected_outputs) instead of the entire YAML body. Token cost per protocol load is roughly 5-10x cheaper.

tool_route now returns recommended_action (Phase 9) AND why_matched (Phase 11a) on every result. Routing is now self-explanatory — a maintainer reading a router log knows why the protocol was picked AND what the AI should do next.

Audit consolidation: 6 audit_* tools collapsed into tool_audit (operation-dispatcher) + tool_audit_findings + tool_audit_quality_full + tool_discussion_coverage_audit = 4 audit surfaces. The convoluted tool_audit_master / tool_audit_synthesis / tool_audit_step_literature wrappers from baseline are gone. tool_audit returns a maintainer-grade error when called with missing args ('tool_audit requires scope= and dimension=. Valid scopes: step | project | synthesis. See docs/V2_MIGRATION_TABLE.md...').

Path/checkpoint/config/env consolidation: sys_path_create/read/list/finalize/branch -> sys_path(operation=...). sys_config_set/get/list -> sys_config. Multiple sys_env_* -> sys_env. Deprecated callsites return a clear migration message: "sys_path_create: renamed to sys_path in v1.6.1, removed in v2.0.0; call sys_path(operation='create', ...) instead." That is exactly the structured error a maintainer wants — no more guessing.

Protocol pack overlap PARTIALLY resolved: protocol IDs are now disjoint between core (methodology/qualitative_*) and the qualitative pack (qualitative/method/*, qualitative/coding/*, qualitative/validity/*, qualitative/output/*). Baseline complained about ID collisions; that specific complaint is fixed. (Trigger-phrase collisions remain — see friction_items.)

Never-called tool list cleaned: 11 of 14 baseline never-called tools are gone (tool_audit_step_completeness, tool_audit_synthesis, tool_audit_master, tool_audit_step_literature, tool_audit_figure_full, tool_figure_palette, tool_preregister_diff, tool_step_env_lock, tool_env_snapshot, tool_env_docker_generate, tool_paper_figures_autoembed, tool_dashboard_create).

scope_tags + pack annotations present: 77+ core protocols now carry scope_tags (domain, audience, workflow_shape) and every tool def carries pack=<name>. Surface is now query-able by scope, which is the foundation for v2.1.0 protocol-pack opt-in registration.

docs/V2_MIGRATION_TABLE.md (20.9K) shipped — the consolidated tool_audit error literally cites this file, so users hitting a migration error have a documented escape hatch. PROTOCOL_DOCTRINE.md (12.3K) is up to date.
remaining_recommendations: Add a preflight that fails if a trigger phrase is registered in BOTH core _router_index.yaml AND any installed pack's router_entries.py. Document trigger-precedence in PROTOCOL_DOCTRINE.md (pack wins when ambiguous, since packs are opt-in and topic-specific).

Standardize the error envelope across handlers: add a small dispatch-level validator that catches 'missing required argument' BEFORE the handler runs and returns {status:'error', code:'missing_required_arg', message:'<friendly>', suggested_actions:[]}. Pilot on tool_python_exec + tool_qualitative_codebook_diff + tool_qualitative_quote_provenance + tool_qualitative_select_standard, then propagate. Use tool_audit's error message as the model.

Fix or document tool_python_exec's cwd semantics. Subprocess cwd is currently the script's parent dir (silent) — switch to workspace root OR document loudly and export WORKSPACE_ROOT env var so scripts have a stable anchor.

v2.1.0 consolidation Round 2: collapse tool_synthesize_plan + tool_synthesize + tool_synthesis_preview + tool_synthesis_curate_figures into tool_synthesize(operation=plan|run|preview|curate_figures). Collapse tool_route + tool_quick_route + tool_research_method + tool_research_tool into tool_route(mode=full|quick|method|tool). Keep tool_semantic_route + sys_semantic_tool_search distinct.
Move tool_poster_create, tool_latex_compile, tool_workflow_dag into an opt-in 'publishing' / 'compute' pack so they don't appear in the surface for qualitative-only workspaces.
Gate adapter_* tool registration on detected installed adapters (sys_adapters_installed returning non-empty). Saves ~4 surface entries in a default workspace.

Ship the planned first-class MCP wrapper for PII redaction (e.g. tool_qualitative_pii_redact) so methodology/qualitative_pii_redaction stops pointing at a researcher-authored script. The quality_bar in that protocol enforces HIPAA 18-class + NER recall >= 0.95 — a maintained reference implementation is needed.
Add protocol_category to reliability.jsonl + a research-os doctor --by-category flag + docs/MAINTAINER_DASHBOARD.md. This was an open ask in baseline; still open.
Write docs/QUALITATIVE_GLOSSARY.md and link it from pack protocol descriptions. This was an open ask in baseline; still open.

Decide pack-version policy: either (a) bump pack protocol versions on every core release, OR (b) document independent pack SemVer in PROTOCOL_DOCTRINE.md. Pack protocols still show 1.9.x in some places while core is on its 2.0.0 cycle.
